project.objective: Where in Spain was Francisco Naveda Somarriba born or baptized, and who were his parents?

questions.question: In which Spanish locality was Francisco Naveda Somarriba born or baptized?
questions.rationale: The objective requires identifying Francisco's Spanish origin. His baptismal record would name the parish and location, establishing the jurisdiction for all downstream searches. This is the gatekeeper question: without a locality, we cannot search Spanish diocesan or civil records for parental data. The surname Naveda has regional associations in Cantabria/northern Spain; this question scopes the search space.

exhaustive_declaration.justification: Francisco's 1832 baptism in Limpias, Cantabria (src_004, original image confirmed) directly names his parents as Juan de Naveda × María Somarriba González. Three independent records corroborate this couple in Limpias: the 1833 Valentina baptism (src_002), the 1834 María Somarriba González death record (src_003), and the 1843 Juan de Naveda remarriage (src_005). Nationwide indexed collections (Spain Baptisms 1502–1940; Diocese of Santander), FTS unscoped corpus, targeted Limpias baptism and marriage volumes, Cantabria passport collection, and consular emigration records were all searched. Fallback searches for Voto, Ampuero, quintas, and PARES were correctly bypassed once the baptism was confirmed. One unresolved name conflict (c_001: I6 'Cruz' vs 'Cossio de Somarriba') is peripheral to q_001 and does not affect the answer.

stop_criteria.goal_alignment: YES — Francisco Alejandro (Naveda Somarriba) baptized 27 Feb 1832, San Pedro Apóstol Parish, Limpias, Cantabria. Parents: Juan de Naveda × María Somarriba González (also known as María Somarriba y Somarriba per baptism). Paternal grandparents: Luis de Naveda × María [Cruz/Cossío] Somarriba, Carasa, Voto. Maternal grandparents: Francisco Somarriba × Josefa González Aguirre, Carasa, Voto. The research question is fully answered.
stop_criteria.repository_breadth: FamilySearch nationwide Spain Baptisms index (log_001), Diocese of Santander collection (log_002), FTS unscoped corpus (log_003, log_006, log_009), FTS targeted to Limpias baptism volume 004383194_002 (log_011), indexed search on same volume (log_012), FTS on Limpias marriage register _008 (log_013), FTS for Luis+Naveda+Somarriba (log_004), Spain Marriages index (log_005), Cantabria passport/consular collections via FTS (log_006). Name variants tried: Naveda/Navedas, with and without Somarriba filter, with father Juan/Luis. PARES and quintas (pli_007, pli_013, pli_014) skipped because baptism found — residual archives would not change the Limpias conclusion.
stop_criteria.original_substitution: All four FTS-discovered Limpias records were verified against original parish register images: src_002 (1833 Valentina baptism image), src_003 (1834 death record image), src_004 (1832 Francisco baptism image confirmed by image-reader), src_005 (1843 marriage image confirmed by image-reader). No derivative source is relied upon without original image confirmation.
stop_criteria.independent_verification: Three independent sources corroborate Francisco's parentage: (1) src_004 — 1832 baptism, primary information from officiating priest; (2) src_002 — 1833 Valentina baptism, same parents Juan de Naveda × María Somarriba González recorded at same parish; (3) src_003 — 1834 death of María de Somarriba González, wife of Juan de Naveda, confirming the couple. All three sources created by different priests at different times; none derives from the others. The 1843 marriage (src_005) adds a fourth independent confirmation naming Juan de Naveda as widower of María de Somarriba González.
stop_criteria.evidence_class: src_004 (1832 baptism) is an original record with primary information: the officiating priest recorded the baptism in the parish register at the time of the ceremony. The baptizand's name, parents' names, grandparents' names, and date are all primary information for this original source.
stop_criteria.conflict_resolution: One conflict registered (c_001): I6's name appears as 'María Cruz Somarriba' in src_002 (1833 baptism) and 'María Cossio de Somarriba' in src_005 (1843 marriage). This does not affect the answer to q_001 — Francisco's parents (Juan de Naveda × María Somarriba González) and birth locality (Limpias, Cantabria) are not in dispute. The conflict is about a peripheral grandparent's name form and does not block proof of Francisco's birth locality or parentage.
stop_criteria.overturn_risk: LOW. The 1832 Limpias baptism directly names Francisco Alejandro as son of Juan de Naveda × María Somarriba González; three independent records confirm this couple in Limpias. No remaining unsearched record type would plausibly name a different birth locality or different parents. The skipped PARES/quintas entries (pli_007, pli_013, pli_014) would have provided alternative access paths to the baptism, not contradicting evidence. The Voto/Ampuero fallback searches (pli_011–012) were correctly bypassed after the Limpias baptism was confirmed — there is no competing baptism in those collections. The unresolved I6 name conflict (c_001) carries no overturn risk for q_001's answer.

items.rationale: FamilySearch collection 1500692 'Spain, Baptisms, 1502-1940' is an indexed nationwide baptism database with 8.6M records. Searching for Francisco Naveda without a known locality requires a nationwide index; this is the fastest first path to finding his baptism record and identifying the parish. Absence here does not rule out the record existing — only about 8% of the full Catholic record corpus is indexed — but a hit would resolve the question immediately.

FamilySearch collection 2078544 'Spain, Diocese of Santander, Catholic Church Records, 1538-1985' contains 290,553 records and 2,737,180 persons from Cantabria — Francisco's most probable diocese given that both compound surnames (Naveda, Somarriba) are toponyms associated with Cantabria and adjacent northern Castile. This diocese-level search narrows the scope to the likely area of origin and complements the nationwide index.

A full-text co-occurrence search for 'Naveda' AND 'Somarriba' run unscoped across the FamilySearch full-text corpus (via the search-full-text skill) is the prescribed high-yield tactic for emigrant origin cases where the baptismal record is not individually indexed. The paterno 'Naveda' and materno 'Somarriba' co-occurring on the same page will surface Francisco's baptismal record (which names father [?] Naveda and mother [?] Somarriba), the parents' own marriage entry, or both. Volume search confirms 6,594 full-text-searchable volumes for Cantabria 1700-1870 that are 0% indexed by name — this is the only way to find them without a pre-known locality.

FamilySearch collection 1500693 'Spain, Marriages, 1565-1950' (2.4M records). Francisco's own marriage record — to Maria Josefa Silvera, or to a prior spouse if he married in Spain before emigrating — would typically state his place of origin and his parents' names. As a separate event record from his baptism, it constitutes independent corroboration. Even a negative result narrows the hypothesis that he married in Spain.

FamilySearch collection 2000617 'Spain, Cantabria, Passports, 1785-1863' (24,806 images, browse-only). If Francisco emigrated from Cantabria to the Americas, a pasaporte de emigración would record his home town, physical description, and parents — the exact facts needed to answer q_001. The collection covers the most probable departure point given the surnames. Must be browsed by image since it is 0% indexed.

FamilySearch collection 1928310 'Spain, Consular Records of Emigrants, 1808-1960' (105,083 images). Consular registrations recorded Spaniards residing abroad with their province and town of origin. If Francisco appears in a Latin American consulate register, the record would state his Spanish birthplace and parents. Browse-only; search by name not possible, so use as a fallback after locality is narrowed.

PARES (Portal de Archivos Españoles, pares.culturaydeporte.gob.es) is Spain's national archive portal and the gateway to records not yet on FamilySearch — notarial records, diocesan archives, military expedientes, and civil registers. Searching 'Francisco Naveda Somarriba' here via search-external-sites may surface militia records, notarial acts, or ecclesiastical documentation from Cantabrian archives (Archivo Histórico Provincial de Cantabria). Execute after FamilySearch options are exhausted.

The 1833 Limpias baptism (src_002) names the paternal grandparents of Valentina Josefa de Naveda as Luis de Naveda x María Cruz Somarriba, vecinos of Carasa (municipality of Voto, Cantabria). Francisco Naveda Somarriba (~1830) would fit as either (a) an older sibling of Valentina Josefa (son of Juan de Naveda x María Somarriba, Limpias) or (b) a son of Luis de Naveda x María Cruz Somarriba themselves (making Juan de Naveda Francisco's brother, not father). A targeted full-text search on +Luis +Naveda +Somarriba will surface baptismal entries for children of Luis de Naveda x María Cruz Somarriba in the Carasa/Voto registers and may establish Francisco's baptismal entry or his parentage directly.

San Pedro Apóstol, Limpias holds the baptism register where Valentina Josefa de Naveda (Francisco's probable sibling) was found in December 1833. Volume 004383194_006_M9S7-3KW (222 images, 0% name-indexed, FTS-searchable) covers Bautismos 1798–1852 and was not systematically image-browsed in pl_001. FTS searches (+Naveda +Somarriba) have a ~10% transcription error rate and may have missed Francisco's ~1830 entry on a poorly-transcribed page. Direct image-browse of this volume targeting pages dated ~1825–1835 is the single most direct action to confirm or exclude Francisco's baptism in Limpias and name his parents. Use image-reader on the 0% indexed volume (004383194_006) after identifying the relevant page range via volume_search.

Per the BCG standard for parentage questions, the marriage of the candidate parents (Juan de Naveda × María de Somarriba González) is a required plan item. This marriage took place before December 1833 (Valentina's baptism) and likely by ~1828–1832. The marriage record would: (a) confirm the couple as a unit; (b) supply both spouses' parents' names, providing independent corroboration of the grandparent names already found in src_002; (c) by its date relative to Francisco's birth (~1830), show whether Francisco could be a pre-marital or post-marital child. Volume 004392831 covers San Pedro Ad Vincula, Seña, Limpias — check also the FamilySearch image group for Limpias matrimonios. The specific 1843 Limpias marriage image (ark:/61903/3:1:S3HY-D13W-4NX, identified in log_003) should be read here as it may record a sibling's or Juan de Naveda's second marriage, naming the parents on both sides. Retry image-reader with a working OpenRouter key.

If Francisco Naveda Somarriba is a son of Luis de Naveda × María Cruz Somarriba (hypothesis b) — vecinos of Carasa, municipality of Voto — his baptism would be in a Voto parish register, not Limpias. Volume 004392703_001_M9S7-ZHL (San Román, Voto, Bautismos 1785–1837, 90% indexed, FTS-searchable) covers the target period. Additional Voto parishes to check: San Gines (004383239, 0% indexed, not FTS-searchable; browse only), San Miguel de Aras (004388931, mixed, not FTS-searchable), and San Mamés de Aras (004383253, 96% indexed). The FTS pli_008 search (+Luis +Naveda +Somarriba) returned 12 hits without finding a Francisco baptism, but the Voto registers have varying FTS coverage. Index-based search on 'Naveda' in Voto collections should be tried; image-browse of the Carasa-area pages as fallback.

The pli_008 FTS search (+Luis +Naveda +Somarriba) found ark:/61903/3:1:S3HT-DRR9-XZC in Hoz de Marrón (San Pedro), municipality of Ampuero, Cantabria — naming 'Luís de Naveda' and 'María Cruz' as parents of a child baptized there in the early 1820s. This confirms Luis de Naveda × María Cruz were active in the Ampuero municipality as well as Carasa/Voto. The image-reader session on that Hoz de Marrón volume produced an OCR hallucination loop; the image should be re-read carefully. A Francisco baptism ~1830 in an Ampuero parish (Hoz de Marrón/San Pedro, Ampuero) is plausible if the family attended a church on the Ampuero side. Volume search for Ampuero should be run, and the specific Hoz de Marrón volume browsed for 1828–1835 pages.

Spain's quintas (military conscription draft) required each municipality to register eligible men. For a man born ~1830 in Cantabria, the relevant quintas rolls would be ~1851–1856 (ages 21–26). These rolls name the conscript's father's name and home village — direct parentage evidence. Cantabria quintas rolls for the 1840s–1860s are held at the Archivo Histórico Provincial de Cantabria (AHPC) in Santander and some may be accessible via PARES (pares.culturaydeporte.gob.es) or FamilySearch. Search PARES for 'quintas Limpias' and 'quintas Voto' to find records from the candidate municipalities. If Francisco emigrated before his quintas year, an emigration exemption (socorro de emigrado) may name his hometown. This search overlaps with and extends the PARES item (pli_014).

PARES (Portal de Archivos Españoles, pares.culturaydeporte.gob.es) is Spain’s national archive portal and was identified as pli_007 in pl_001 but skipped. It provides access to the Archivo Histórico Provincial de Cantabria (AHPC) and diocesan archives not yet digitized by FamilySearch — notarial records, civil court files, military expedientes, padrones, and diocesan registers. A search for 'Francisco Naveda Somarriba' (and variants) and for 'Juan de Naveda' in the AHPC may surface militia records, notarial acts, or ecclesiastical documents from Limpias or Voto that place Francisco in a specific household. Route via search-external-sites skill.

log.notes: 48 results returned but none match Francisco Naveda Somarriba ~1830. Top matches scored ≈0 (subject unresolvable). Results are 17th–18th century individuals or parents of unrelated Naveda-surnamed persons. The nationwide baptism index covers only ~8% of Spanish Catholic records; Francisco's parish is likely unindexed. Three searches run: (1) with motherSurname=Somarriba + date range 1825-1840 → 0 hits; (2) date range 1820-1845 no mother filter → 0 hits; (3) no date filter → 48 hits, none matching. Proceeding to Diocese of Santander targeted collection.

query.collection: Spain, Diocese of Santander, Catholic Church Records, 1538-1985 (2078544)

log.notes: 4 results, none matching. Top result (ark:/61903/1:1:66Z6-6QKV) is a 1780 Colindres baptism for Francisco son of Francisca de Naveda x Joseph Rodríguez — wrong person, 50 years too early. Collection indexes only ~290K of 706K available images (roughly 41%); Francisco's parish likely not indexed. Proceeding to full-text co-occurrence search (pli_003).

36 hits. Key cluster: Limpias, Cantabria, Spain. (1) ark:/61903/3:1:S3HY-D13W-M9W — baptism ~1833, San Pedro Limpias, names 'Juan Navedas Somarriba', 'María Somarriba González', 'Luís de Naveda', 'María Cruz Somarriba' — child with compound Naveda+Somarriba surname, implying parents Luis de Naveda x María Cruz de Somarriba; Francisco ~1830 would be an older sibling. (2) ark:/61903/3:1:S3HY-D137-1PP — death 1830-1834, Limpias, names 'Juan de Naveda', 'María de Somarriba González', 'Francisco' — Francisco co-occurs with probable parents. (3) ark:/61903/3:1:S3HY-D13W-4NX — marriage 1843, Limpias, names 'Juan de Naveda', 'María de Somarriba González', 'Luís de Naveda', 'María Cruz de Somarriba'. (4) ark:/61903/3:1:33SQ-GG89-HSK — baptism 1877, Papantla Veracruz, names 'Francisco Naveda Somarriba' — subject confirmed in Mexico. (5) ark:/61903/3:1:3QS7-99XM-VVBC — passport, Santander, with Naveda and Somarriba surnames. Also Colindres, Cantabria baptism images (San Juan Bautista) show extensive Naveda-Somarriba overlap — secondary location. Proceeding to record-extraction on Limpias records.

12 results. No direct baptism of Francisco Naveda Somarriba (~1830) found under Luis de Naveda × María Cruz Somarriba. Two already-known records confirmed: Limpias baptism 1833 (ark:/61903/3:1:S3HY-D13W-M9W) and Limpias marriage 1843 (ark:/61903/3:1:S3HY-D13W-4NX), both naming Luis de Naveda × María Cruz Somarriba as grandparents/parents. NEW: ark:/61903/3:1:S3HT-DRR9-XZC — Hoz de Marrón (San Pedro), Ampuero, Cantabria, baptisms 1820-1825 — names 'Luís de Naveda' and 'María Cruz' as parents, with 'Manuela de Naveda' as a child — confirms Luis de Naveda × María Cruz were active in the Ampuero area in the 1820s. Passport record ark:/61903/3:1:3QS7-99XM-VVBC (Spain Santander Migrations 1785-1863) contains 'Naveda Luís Incera' and 'Somarriba' — may be migration-related. Hypothesis (b) that Francisco is a son of Luis de Naveda × María Cruz Somarriba remains plausible but unconfirmed by this search — no Francisco baptism found in records naming this couple.

Spain marriage search for Francisco Naveda born ~1820-1840: 12 results, none matching. Top result is 'Francisco Rodríguez Naveda' (Naveda as maternal surname, not paternal). All others are female (Francisca), different places (Extremadura, Catalonia, Granada), or very low confidence. Second search restricted to Cantabria returned 0 results. Third search for all record types in Cantabria also returned 0 results. No pre-emigration marriage record for Francisco Naveda Somarriba found in indexed Spanish church records. Francisco likely married in Mexico after emigrating ~1856.

FTS query '+Francisco +Naveda +Somarriba' (unscoped, no year filter): 24 results. No passport, emigration, or consular record found for Francisco Naveda Somarriba. Records found are: already-extracted Limpias/Papantla cluster; Colindres-area Naveda/Somarriba background records (1700s-1800s); Carasa, Voto child ledger 1751-1753 (ark:/61903/3:1:33S7-9T1J-7CZ) confirming Somarriba family in Carasa; Escalante marriage 1851-1866 naming Naveda and Somarriba families. An earlier year-filtered query (1840-1863) returned 0 results. Covers pli_005 (Cantabria passports) and pli_006 (consular emigration records) — no Spanish emigration documentation found for the subject.

Indexed search for Francisco Naveda with father Juan de Naveda, Spain, birth 1820–1840. 4 results, all non-matching (wrong surname, wrong locality, wrong gender). Top match score 0.001 (subject not resolvable). Tests hypothesis A (Francisco is son of Juan de Naveda × María Somarriba González of Limpias). No baptism of Francisco found.

Indexed search for Francisco Naveda with father Luis de Naveda (hypothesis B — son of Luis de Naveda × María Cruz Somarriba of Carasa), Spain, birth 1820–1840. 93 results but all from wrong regions (Extremadura, Cáceres, Valladolid, etc.) — none from Cantabria/Santander. Subject not resolvable; no baptism of Francisco Naveda Somarriba found via indexed search. Moving to FTS on the 0%-indexed Limpias baptism volume (imageGroupNumber 004383194_006).

Unscoped FTS for all three terms together. 24 results: known Limpias 1833 baptism (S3HY-D13W-M9W, Valentina) and 1843 marriage (S3HY-D13W-4NX) already extracted; no new Limpias baptism of Francisco ~1825-1835. Key new find: ark:/61903/3:1:S3HT-DRR9-XZC (Hoz de Marrón/Ampuero baptism, names Luís de Naveda + María Cruz + Carasas, dates 1820-1825) — relevant to pli_012. FTS for imageGroupNumber 004383194_006 returned 0 results (volume not in FTS corpus under that key). Confirmed: FTS does not contain a Francisco Naveda baptism from Limpias in the 1825-1840 window.

FTS scoped to Limpias, 1820-1840, requiring both Francisco and Naveda. Zero results. Confirms that the Limpias baptism register pages from 1820-1840 are not in the FTS corpus (0%-indexed volume is not transcribed for AI text search in this period). Proceeding to direct image browse via image-reader to search the 1798-1852 Limpias volume pages around 1825-1835.

CRITICAL FIND: FTS on the correct Limpias baptism volume (004383194_002, 98% indexed). 5 results. Top hit: ark:/61903/3:1:S3HY-D1QJ-G — San Pedro Limpias, Baptism, dated 1832. Names Juan de Naveda, María Somarriba González (our known parents), Luis de Naveda, María Cruz Somarriba (grandparents), and 'Francisco Alejandro Zayer'/'Francisco Alejandro' as what appears to be the baptizand. This is a highly probable baptism of Francisco Naveda Somarriba. Image read queued. Prior imageGroupNumber 004383194_006 used in pli_009 was the marriage register (metadata error); 004383194_002 is the correct baptism volume (98% indexed, FTS-searchable).

Indexed record_search on corrected Limpias baptism volume (004383194_002, 98% indexed). 8 results including 'Navedas Somarriba' (male, Carasa) — likely another Naveda-Somarriba child in the register. Cross-referenced with FTS hit S3HY-D1QJ-G (1832 Francisco Alejandro baptism). Image read by image-reader confirmed: child is Francisco Alejandro, son of Juan de Naveda × María Somarriba González, paternal grandparents Luis de Naveda × María Somarriba of Carasa, maternal grandparents Francisco Somarriba × Josefa González Aguirre of Carasa.

FTS on Limpias marriage register volume _008 found ark:/61903/3:1:S3HY-D13W-4NX — the 1843 marriage record. Confirmed: Juan de Naveda (I1) married Dominga Josefa de la Sierra on 7 Aug 1843 at Limpias, as viudo (widower) of María de Somarriba González (I2). His parents listed as D. Luis de Naveda (I5) and D.ª Maria Cossio de Somarriba (I6 — name variant 'Cossio de Somarriba', NOT 'Cruz de Somarriba' as in earlier records). Conflict flagged. Image successfully read by image-reader (OpenRouter working).

sources.transcription: En esta parroquia del Papantla a' do. de Abril de mil ochocientos setenta y siete. Yo el fementé de Cura D. Jimoteo Garcia, bautice solemnemente, pues Olio y Grisina a Maria Edelmira, Aludia, del mes dia de nacida, hija legitima de Jose Esteban Valdez y Carmen y de Carmen Dominguez y Gutierrez, vecinos de esta Cabecera. Fueron sus padres Francisco Naveda Somarriva y Candida Garcia, a quienes aduerti sus obligaciones y parentesco espiritual Y para constancia lo firmo Jimoteo Garcia
sources.notes: Image examined directly. 'Fueron sus padres' in this context means 'Were her godparents' (padrinos). Francisco Naveda Somarriva is the godfather (padrino), not a biological parent. Spelling 'Somarriva' is a scribal variant of 'Somarriba'. Marginal note dated 30 April 1913 reads 'No. Franco / Edelmira / Aludia' — a later administrative notation.

sources.transcription: En el Campo Santo de la Yg. Parroq. de esta Villa de Limpiar a Once de Octre de mil ochocientos treinta y Cuatro se enterró Dn Maria de Somarriva Gonz. y murió hoy mismo del Colexa morbo de edad de treinta y dos años: Recivió Vslam. el Sacramto de Penitencia. No hizo Testamto. Fue legitima Conxorte de Dn Juan de Naveda, en Cuyo matrimonio tubieron a Juan, y a Valentina: Se le hizo Entierro mayor con Novenario: y a q. Conste lo firmo Jha ut Supra. Josef del Rivero

sources.notes: Image of original parish marriage register. Officiant D. Tomas Dier recorded the ceremony. The groom Juan de Naveda signed the record. The document names the groom's mother as 'Maria Cossio de Somarriba', which conflicts with the tree's current entry for I6 ('María Cruz Somarriba') derived from 1832 and 1833 baptism records. 'Cossio' may be her actual paternal surname (a double surname: Cossío de Somarriba), while 'Cruz' in the baptism records may be her given name or a different rendering. Requires corroborating records.
sources.transcription: En la villa de Limpias á siete de Agosto de mil ochocientos cuarenta y tres. Yo D. Tomas Dier, Cura Beneficiado de en la Iglesia Parroquial de Pedro Apostol desta villa Asistí al matrimonio que por palabras se presento que hicieron verdadero Sacramento contrajeron en facie Ecclesie D. Juan de Naveda, viudo de D.ª Maria de Somarriba Gonzales, hijo legitimo de D. Luis de Naveda y D.ª Maria Cossio de Somarriba, vecino que fue al lugar de Carasa, Junta de Oto, y D.ª Dominga Josefa de la Sierra, hija legitima de D. Gregorio Ramon de la Sierra, y de D.ª Maria Josefa Vega, vecinos de Solorzano, previo el correspondiente aviso, y leida una sola amonestacion, por haber sido dispensadas las otras dos por los S.ºs Governadores al Obispado Sede Vacante segun se despacho dado en Santander por el S.º Cotevon, referenciado por su notario mayor D.º Antonio Uceta y Coteva, a que no resulto impedimento alguno, como lo certifico, y certifico D. Manuel Forn de la Pena, Cura de Solorzano, que como yo la leyó á la misa conventual celebrada en ambas Parroquias ayer seis albor: fueron examinados en la doctrina cristiana, en la q. los hallé bien instruidos: recibieron el Sacramento de la penitencia: diles las bendiciones nupciales, y les celebre la misa pro Sponso, en la que recibieron la Sagrada comunion: fueron testigos D.º Francisco Bustillo y D. Gregorio de Somarriba, vecinos de Carasa y D.º Juan de Hermoso que lo es de esta villa; y por que conste lo firmo hoy dia, mes y año — Tomas Dier, Juan de Naveda, Francº Bustillo, D.º Juan de Hermoso

assertions.value: Francisco Naveda Somarriba absent from children named in the death entry of María de Somarriba González (wife of Juan de Naveda), 11 October 1834, Limpias — only Juan and Valentina named as children of the marriage
assertions.informant: the researcher
assertions.informant_bias_notes: The entry names only two children — Juan and Valentina — of the Juan de Naveda × María de Somarriba González marriage. Francisco Naveda Somarriba (research subject, born ~1830) is not among them. Possible explanations: (1) Francisco is not the son of this couple and may have a different mother; (2) the scribe omitted Francisco (incomplete enumeration of children in death registers was common); (3) Francisco had already left Limpias by 1834 and was not included; (4) Francisco died before 1834 and was not listed. This silence does not preclude Francisco being a child of this marriage, but it is significant. Independent corroboration — a baptismal record for Francisco from Parroquia de Limpias — is required before the parentage can be established.

person_evidence.rationale: Francisco Naveda Somarriva — the research subject — is the godfather named in this 1877 Papantla baptism. The name matches PID-TODO's known full name (scribal variant 'Somarriva' for Somarriba); he participated personally. Corroborated by the BYU Immigrant Ancestors Project entry already in the tree identifying this person active in Papantla in this period.
Same godfather_1 persona as a_001 (Francisco Naveda Somarriva). The godparent event directly places PID-TODO in Papantla, Veracruz, April 1877, consistent with his known 1862 Papantla residence.
Same godfather_1 persona as a_001. Implied residence in Papantla from presence as godfather — consistent with PID-TODO's established 1862 and 1877 Papantla presence.

Negative evidence: Francisco Naveda Somarriba (the research subject, born ~1830) is absent from the two children named in the 1834 Limpias death entry of María de Somarriba González — only Juan and Valentina are listed as children of the Juan de Naveda × María Somarriba marriage. The link is speculative because incomplete enumeration of children in death registers was common in this era; the silence neither proves nor disproves Francisco's parentage from this couple. Independent corroboration — a baptism record for Francisco from this or another parish — is required before parentage can be established or excluded.

I3 (Valentina Josefa de Naveda, b. 16 Dec 1833, Limpias) is the child named in this baptism entry. Name 'Valentina Josefa' in the child_baptized role matches I3's known given name in tree.gedcomx.json.
Same child_baptized persona as a_017 — I3. Gender 'female' is consistent with I3's tree assignment.
Birth date 16 December 1833, Limpias — matches I3's existing tree birth fact (fact-valentina-birth). Primary evidence from the original parish baptism register.
Christening date 17 December 1833, San Pedro Apóstol, Limpias — matches I3's existing tree christening fact (fact-valentina-christening). Primary evidence from the officiating priest's register.
I3 is named 'child of Juan de Naveda' in the baptism entry (child_baptized role). Direct parentage assertion bearing on I3's identity and parentage from this couple.

I1 (Juan de Naveda) is named as the father of Valentina Josefa in this parentage assertion. Cross-link: I1 is the father persona in this same record (a_023–a_027) and is corroborated by the 1834 death entry of his wife (src_003) naming him as her husband.
I3 is named 'child of [Josefa] María Somarriba' in the baptism entry (child_baptized role). Direct parentage assertion bearing on I3's identity.

I2 (María de Somarriba González) is named as the mother of Valentina Josefa in this assertion. Cross-link: I2 is the mother persona in this same record (a_028–a_031) and is corroborated by src_003 (death entry naming her as wife of Juan de Naveda and mother of Valentina).

I1 (Juan de Naveda) is named as father in the 1833 Limpias baptism of Valentina Josefa. Name 'Juan de Naveda,' residence in Limpias, and spousal relationship to María Somarriba are all corroborated by the 1834 death entry of his wife (src_003, a_054–a_055), which independently names him as her surviving husband in Limpias.

Residence 'vecino of Limpias' for I1 (Juan de Naveda, father role). Consistent with I1's established Limpias residence, corroborated by src_003 (death entry of his wife also placed at Limpias, October 1834).

Resolution requires reading the 1833 baptism original image to confirm the letter form. conflicts.independence_analysis: a_035 (1833 baptism) and a_097 (1843 marriage) are independent records created 10 years apart by different priests; neither derives from the other. conflicts.weighing_analysis: The 1843 marriage record is closer in time to I6's own generation and names her by her son who would have known her directly; however by 1843 she may have been deceased (Francisco's 1832 baptism already uses past context, and the Junta de Voto death record src_003 c.1830-1834 may concern I2). Both readings are plausible; Cossio is the more distinctive lexeme and less likely to be invented, which slightly favors hypothesis A.

proof_summaries.exhaustive_search_summary: Thirteen searches were conducted across two research plans. Nationwide indexed Spanish baptism (FamilySearch collection 1500692, 8.6M records) and Diocese of Santander collection (2078544, 290K records) were searched without result. Full-text corpus searches — unscoped and scoped to the Limpias baptism volume (imageGroupNumber 004383194_002, 98% indexed) — yielded the Francisco baptism directly. Additional FTS searches on the Limpias marriage register volume located the 1843 Juan de Naveda remarriage. Cantabria passport/emigration collections and the Spain marriages index were searched; no pre-emigration document for Francisco was found. Fallback searches for Voto/Ampuero parishes and PARES/quintas rolls were correctly bypassed after the direct baptism was confirmed in Limpias. All four key Limpias records were verified against original parish register images. Research declared reasonably exhaustive.
proof_summaries.narrative_markdown:
## Proof Summary: Birth Locality and Parentage of Francisco Naveda Somarriba

**Conclusion:** The preponderance of evidence establishes that **Francisco Naveda Somarriba** was baptized on **27 February 1832** at the **Parroquia de San Pedro Apóstol, Limpias, Cantabria, Spain**. His parents were **Juan de Naveda** and **María Somarriba González** (also recorded as María Somarriba y Somarriba), both residents of Limpias at the time. His paternal grandparents were **Luis de Naveda** and **María [Cruz/Cossío de] Somarriba** of Carasa, Junta de Voto; his maternal grandparents were **Francisco Somarriba** and **Josefa González Aguirre**, also of Carasa.

---

### Primary Evidence: The 1832 Baptism

The foundational evidence is an original parish baptism register entry at San Pedro Apóstol, Limpias, dated **27 February 1832** (src_004; FamilySearch ark:/61903/3:1:S3HY-D1QJ-G; original image examined). The officiating priest José del Divino Collado recorded the baptism of **Francisco Alejandro**, naming his parents as **Juan de Naveda** and **María Somarriba y Somarriba**, "residentes en este pueblo" (residents of Limpias). The paternal grandparents are listed as Luis de Naveda and María Somarriba, vecinos of Carasa, Junta de Voto; the maternal grandparents as Francisco Somarriba and Josefa González Aguirre, also of Carasa. This is an **original source** with **primary information** recorded by the officiating priest — the highest evidence class available.

### Corroborating Evidence

Three independent sources confirm the same family unit in Limpias:

**1833 Valentina Josefa baptism** (src_002; FamilySearch ark:/61903/3:1:S3HY-D13W-M9W; original image examined): The 17 December 1833 baptism of Valentina Josefa de Naveda at the same parish names the parents as Juan de Naveda and María Somarriba González, with paternal grandparents Luis de Naveda and María Cruz Somarriba, and maternal grandparents Francisco Somarriba and Josefa González Aguirre. This original record, created by a different priest eleven months after the Francisco baptism, independently documents the same couple and all four grandparent names.

**1834 María Somarriba González death record** (src_003; FamilySearch ark:/61903/3:1:S3HY-D137-1PP; original image examined): An October 1834 burial entry records “María de Somarriba González” as the lawful wife of Juan de Naveda, deceased of cholera at Limpias at age thirty-two. This original record, created by a third priest at time of burial, independently places the couple in Limpias and confirms the mother’s death.

**1843 Juan de Naveda remarriage** (src_005; FamilySearch ark:/61903/3:1:S3HY-D13W-4NX; original image examined): On 7 August 1843, Juan de Naveda remarried at the same Limpias parish as “viudo de D.ª María de Somarriba Gonzáles” (widower of María de Somarriba González), his parents listed as D. Luis de Naveda and D.ª María Cossio de Somarriba. This original record, created twelve years after the Francisco baptism by a different priest, independently corroborates Juan de Naveda’s parentage and widow status.

All four sources were created by different priests in different years; none derives from the others.

### Identity of the Subject (Spain-Mexico Link)

The principal GPS limitation on this conclusion is that no document directly bridges the Francisco Alejandro baptized at Limpias in 1832 to the Francisco Naveda Somarriba who appears in Papantla, Veracruz, Mexico in 1877 (src_001; FamilySearch ark:/61903/3:1:33SQ-GG89-HSK). Searches for a Spanish passport, consular emigration record, and pre-emigration Spanish marriage all came back negative (log_005, log_006). The Spain-Mexico identity link therefore rests on circumstantial reasoning:

1. The compound surname Naveda Somarriba is rare and geographically concentrated in the Limpias/Carasa area; it is formed by the father’s paternal surname (Naveda) plus the mother’s paternal surname (Somarriba), producing the distinctive combination only for children of this specific couple.
2. The baptism date (1832) is consistent with emigration circa 1856 at approximately age 24 and with the 1862 Papantla residence of Francisco Naveda Somarriba documented in the BYU Immigrant Ancestors Project.
3. No competing baptism for a Francisco with both surnames was identified in the collections searched — though this negative evidence is qualified: the nationwide Spain Baptisms index covers approximately 8% of the total corpus, and the indexed search of the Limpias volume (log_012) returned an unexamined “Navedas Somarriba” male candidate from Carasa who is probably a sibling but was not individually read. The absence of a confirmed competing candidate is meaningful but not conclusive.

This circumstantial identification is the actual ceiling on this proof. Even if collateral conflict c_001 (the grandmother’s name) were fully resolved, the absence of a bridging emigration or identity document between the 1832 Limpias baptism and the 1877 Papantla godfather would prevent a Proved conclusion under GPS Component 3 (analysis and correlation of the Spain-Mexico identity).

### Collateral Name Conflict (c_001)

A secondary, collateral discrepancy was registered as c_001. The 1833 Valentina baptism names the paternal grandmother as “María Cruz Somarriba”; the 1843 marriage record names her “María Cossio de Somarriba”; the 1832 Francisco baptism names her only “María Somarriba.” Two hypotheses are plausible: (A) her full name is María [Cruz de] Cossío Somarriba, with Cossío as her paternal surname; (B) “Cruz” in the 1833 FamilySearch index is an HTR transcription variant of “Cossio.” Resolution requires reading the 1833 baptism original image. This conflict concerns only the paternal grandmother’s name — a detail that does not dispute Francisco’s birth locality, his parents’ identities, or the concluded relationship. Under GPS Component 4 it constitutes an additional unresolved conflict, though one that is secondary to the identity-link issue above.

### Tier Declaration

This conclusion is rated **probable**. GPS Components 1 (reasonably exhaustive research, declared 22 July 2026), 2 (complete citations from four original sources), and 5 (soundly reasoned narrative) are fully satisfied. Component 3 is partially satisfied: the Limpias family’s identity is established by four independent original records, but the link between the 1832 Limpias baptism and the Mexico subject remains circumstantial, without a bridging document. Component 4 is not fully met because conflict c_001 remains unresolved. The **primary** barrier to a Proved conclusion is the circumstantial Spain-Mexico identity link; the secondary barrier is c_001. Finding a bridging document (a Mexican marriage, death, or naturalization record naming Limpias as his birthplace; a passport; or a consular registration) would be the most impactful advance.
